mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following rising-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes broad-based earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect disappears (p > 0.10) when tested on out-of-sample periods after the trigger fires.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deceleration in INDPRO growth alongside stalling PAYEMS growth indicates a simultaneous supply-side and labor-side slowdown that amplifies recession probability beyond what either series signals independently, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0 AND PAYEMS 3-month change < 50k
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 60d forward returns are not statistically distinguishable from either single-series trigger's returns (ANOVA p > 0.10), indicating no incremental interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a decline in HOUST reflects rate-sensitive demand destruction in housing that leads broader consumer spending contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this relationship is muted or absent when policy is accommodative because housing weakness then reflects supply constraints rather than demand destruction.
failure_condition: The negative return effect of falling HOUST is equally strong or stronger during easing regimes (FEDFUNDS declining) than tightening regimes, contradicting the regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month annualized inflation exceeds 4%, markets price in accelerated Fed tightening risk, causing an immediate risk-off repricing that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week (5 trading days) following the print.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following high-inflation prints are not significantly different from unconditional 5-day returns (p > 0.10) across at least 10 triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects ongoing labor income generation supporting consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next week regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly growth and forward 5d returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in RSAFS combined with a stable or falling UNRATE signals synchronized strength in consumer demand and labor conditions that reinforces a positive earnings outlook, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; when RSAFS rises but UNRATE simultaneously rises, the signal is considered unreliable due to conflicting demand-labor dynamics.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.3% AND UNRATE flat or declining
failure_condition: 20d forward returns under the joint condition do not exceed returns under RSAFS-only trigger by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10), indicating no interaction value-add.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime defined by rising FEDFUNDS, an accompanying acceleration in CPIAUCSL inflation signals the Fed is behind the curve and must tighten further than currently priced, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; in an easing regime, the same CPIAUCSL acceleration has no reliable directional effect because policy response expectations differ.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months AND CPIAUCSL YoY inflation accelerating
failure_condition: 60d forward returns following the joint trigger during tightening regimes are not significantly more negative than returns following the same CPIAUCSL acceleration during easing regimes (p > 0.10).
